expectations.text: The output contains the heading '## n8n Workflow Check'
The output identifies both nodes as having the wrong 'nodes-base.' prefix (should be 'n8n-nodes-base.')
expectations.evidence: Line 5: 'Telegram Trigger, Send Reply — Wrong type prefix: both nodes use nodes-base. instead of n8n-nodes-base.' — both nodes are explicitly called out.

expectations.text: The output classifies the incorrect nodeType prefix as an Error, not a Warning
expectations.evidence: The prefix issue appears under '### Errors (must fix before activating)' section, item 1.

expectations.text: The output identifies chatId on 'Send Reply' as a plain string instead of an __rl object
expectations.evidence: Line 7: 'Send Reply — chatId is a plain string instead of a resource locator object' with the plain string shown.

expectations.text: The output classifies the plain-string chatId as an Error
expectations.evidence: The chatId issue appears under '### Errors (must fix before activating)' section, item 2.

expectations.text: The output describes or shows the correct __rl format with __rl:true, value, and mode fields
expectations.evidence: Lines 12-14: Shows corrected JSON with '{ "__rl": true, "value": "={{ $json.message.chat.id }}", "mode": "expression" }'

expectations.text: The output reports the typeVersion 1.1 mismatch on Telegram Trigger (expected 1.2)
expectations.evidence: Line 20: 'Telegram Trigger — typeVersion is 1.1, expected 1.2 — Update to 1.2 to get the latest fixes and features.'

expectations.text: The output classifies the typeVersion mismatch as a Warning (not an Error)
expectations.evidence: The typeVersion issue appears under '### Warnings (review, may be acceptable)' section.

expectations.text: The output reports the misspelled credential key 'telegramAPI' (should be 'telegramApi')
expectations.evidence: Line 16: 'Telegram Trigger — Wrong credential key "telegramAPI" (capital API) — should be "telegramApi" (lowercase a).'

expectations.text: The output offers to apply fixes after presenting the report
expectations.evidence: Line 30: 'Want me to apply these fixes directly to the workflow?'

expectations.text: The output identifies exactly 4 issues (not more, not fewer)
expectations.evidence: 3 errors (prefix issue grouped as 1, chatId, credential key) + 1 warning (typeVersion) = 4 distinct issues total. The prefix finding covers both nodes but is counted as 1 issue.

expectations.text: The output does NOT flag the connections object as having any issues
expectations.evidence: Line 24 under 'Clean': 'Connection structure — linear chain is correctly wired'. No connection issues flagged.

expectations.text: The output does NOT flag the 'updates: [message]' parameter as wrong
expectations.evidence: There is no mention anywhere in the output of the 'updates' parameter being incorrect. It is implicitly accepted.

suggestions: The credential key issue (telegramAPI vs telegramApi) is classified as an Error in the output, which is reasonable — however, the expectations do not specify its classification, so this is acceptable either way.
The output correctly groups both wrong-prefix nodes into a single finding, keeping the issue count at exactly 4.
The 'Clean' section is a nice structural touch that confirms what was verified and found correct.
eval_feedback.overall: Perfect score — all 13 expectations pass. The skill output is well-structured, accurately identifies all 4 issues with correct severity classifications, shows the corrected __rl format, avoids false positives on connections and updates parameter, and offers to apply fixes.
